_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL House Hillsborough/Manatee batch HD-67..71: driskell(D), mcclure(R), alvarez(R), owen(R), robinson-jr(R). Primary-source FL Legislature roll-call PDFs + endorsements. Currency: Driskell term-limited->running FL Senate SD-16; Robinson term-limited NOT running 2026; Owen first elected 2024 (pre-2025 bills N/A).

set.office: State Representative, District 67 (House Democratic Leader; 2026 candidate for FL Senate SD-16)

profile.background: Tampa attorney (Harvard BA, Georgetown JD); FL House Democratic Leader 2024-2026. Represented HD-63 (2018-22), HD-67 after redistricting. Term-limited in House; running for FL Senate District 16 in 2026.

fentrice-driskell@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 from official FL Legislature roll-call PDFs. Uniformly progressive record: NAY on SB 300 (6-wk abortion ban), HB 1557 (Parental Rights in Education), HB 1 (universal vouchers), HB 931 (school chaplains), SB 7050 (elections), SB 1718 (E-Verify/immigration), HB 543 (permitless carry). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood, Ruth's List, EMILY's List (HARD contrary on life/funding). Co-carried bipartisan police-accountability reform HB 7051 (113-0) but no defunding stance. CURRENCY: term-limited in House, filed for FL Senate SD-16 in 2026.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (6-wk ban); endorsed by Planned Parenthood/Ruth's List - documented pro-choice, contrary to personhood-from-conception.

1.note: Voted against even a 6-week restriction; opposes abortion limits, let alone abolition.

2.note: No public position found on embryonic stem-cell/IVF discard.

3.note: No public position found on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: Backed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood/Ruth's List - abortion-industry support.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage definition located.

1.note: No documented vote on same-sex marriage/civil unions in law.

2.note: No specific recorded vote on transgender ideology located (no FL trans-care vote cited for her).

3.note: No position on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 (Parental Rights in Education) - opposed limits on LGBTQ classroom instruction.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 1 universal vouchers; 'nothing in this bill that I like.'

1.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 which included parental notification on gender interventions.

2.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 restricting gender-ideology classroom instruction.

4.note: No position on faith-based adoption agencies located.

0.note: No documented 10A/nullification vote.

set.office: State Representative, District 68 (Chair, House Budget Committee)

profile.background: Plant City business owner (b. 1987); Hillsborough County Farm Bureau. In office since Dec 2017 (HD-58, renumbered HD-68). Chair, House Budget Committee 2024-2026.

lawrence-mcclure@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 from FL Legislature roll-call PDFs and issue-group endorsements. Conservative record: YEA on SB 300 (6-wk ban), HB 1557, SB 254 (trans-care minor ban), HB 1 (universal vouchers), HB 543 (permitless carry). Endorsed by Florida Right to Life (2024) and NRA (A-rating). Voted to lower firearm purchase age to 18. HB 1808 (anti-sanctuary) YEA. No cross-party deviations found.

danny-alvarez@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 from 13 official FL House roll-call PDFs (all 'Alvarez-69 = Y'). Strong conservative: YEA on SB 300 (6-wk ban), HB 1069, SB 254, HB 1521 (bathroom), HB 1 (vouchers), HB 3 (porn age-verify), HB 931 (chaplains), SB 7054 (CBDC ban), SB 7050 (elections), SB 1718 (E-Verify), SB 4-C (2025 illegal-entry state crime), HB 543 (permitless carry), SB 450 (death penalty 8-4 jury). No cross-party deviations.

profile.background: Hillsborough/Manatee (Apollo Beach) real-estate attorney (Saint Leo BA, Cooley JD); former Hillsborough County Commissioner. First elected Nov 2024 (first session 2025) - so all 2022-2024 statewide bills predate his House tenure. Filed for re-election 2026.

michael-owen@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. THIN by tenure: first elected Nov 2024, so pre-2025 marquee bills (SB 300, HB 1069, SB 254, HB 1521, HB 1, HB 3, HB 931, SB 1718, HB 543, SB 7050, SB 7054) are NOT his votes - null, not scored. Confirmed HARD: YEA on SB 4-C and SB 2-C (2025 immigration enforcement, 'Owen-70 = Y'). Florida Right to Life endorsed (2024). Law-enforcement endorsements: Hillsborough Sheriff Chronister, Manatee Sheriff Wells, firefighters. Score reflects documented positions only.

will-robinson-jr@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 from official FL House individual-member vote PDFs. Conservative record with TWO CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay') and HB 1069 (2023 Parental Rights expansion) - against the classroom-instruction restrictions, one of few Republicans to do so. Otherwise YEA: HB 5/SB 300 (abortion bans), SB 254 (trans-care minor ban), HB 1521 (bathroom), HB 1 (vouchers), HB 3 (porn age-verify), HB 931 (chaplains), SB 7054 (CBDC ban), SB 90 + SB 7050 (elections), SB 1718 (E-Verify), HB 543 (permitless carry), HB 1 2021 (anti-riot). NRA-endorsed 2022. CURRENCY: term-limited, not running 2026.
